summary: Self-improving loop on error_envelope.cross_layer for DB_UNAVAILABLE — schema-pure contract test, target 100.
logs: Incident: PG pool calls .end() while connections still queued; next request crashes pg-pool with "Cannot use a pool after calling end on the pool"
parallel[0] failed: agent({schema}): StructuredOutput retry cap (5) exceeded — 5 failed calls with no valid output
parallel[2] failed: agent({schema}): StructuredOutput retry cap (5) exceeded — 5 failed calls with no valid output

spec_fidelity.reason: All spec_anchors satisfied: (1) all must_have_fields present (code, message, messageKey, traceId, traceCode, timestamp, action); (2) code is "DB_UNAVAILABLE"; (3) http_status is 503; (4) action.kind is "retry" (with after:5, matching spec's `{ kind: 'retry', after?: number }`); (5) messageKey "errors.system.dbUnavailable" has prefix "errors.system"; (6) all three required headers present (X-Trace-Id, X-Request-Id, X-Relay-Health); (7) traceCode "R-AGJV" matches the R-XXXX surface form documented in spec (R-3F8K, R-AB42 examples). Incident-appropriate: cause.layer=pg with action=retry matches §8 "After W1-W5" postmortem envelope shape for pg pool failure. timestamp is ISO 8601. X-Relay-Health=degraded correctly broadcasts §4.1.5 degradation signal.

redaction.reason: Redaction audit passed. All string fields scanned for leaks: no raw "Cannot use a pool after calling end on the pool" pg-pool error, no Postgres DSN (postgres://user:pwd@host), no absolute filesystem paths (/Users/, /home/, C:\), no API keys (sk-, OPENROUTER_*, bearer tokens), no stack frames. The cause.message field contains a sanitized generic paraphrase "connection pool unavailable" rather than the raw pg-pool exception text, which satisfies §4.2.3 redaction requirements and §9 G5 gap closure. All spec anchors satisfied: required fields (code, message, messageKey, traceId, traceCode, timestamp, action) present; code=DB_UNAVAILABLE, http_status=503, action.kind=retry, messageKey="errors.system.dbUnavailable" matches prefix; X-Trace-Id, X-Request-Id, X-Relay-Health=degraded headers all present per §4.1.5 global health banner contract. traceCode "R-AGJV" is a 4-char short code per §2.2 format. cause.layer="pg" is within enum per §2.1.

trace.reason: traceCode mismatch. Per api/src/errors.ts:547 `traceCodeFromTraceId`, the code is a deterministic Crockford-base32 fold of the first 40 bits of the traceId hex. For traceId `01935f4e-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-deadbeef1234`: hex[0..10]=`01935f4eaa`, high=0x01935=6453, low=0xf4eaa=1003690, folded=1005263 → Crockford base32 of low 20 bits = `YNWZ`, so the expected traceCode is `R-YNWZ`. The envelope claims `R-AGJV`, which is not a valid derivation. This violates the docs/architecture/error-handling.md §2.2 single-rule that traceCode is the canonical short alias of traceId and breaks the support reverse-lookup contract (errors.ts:537-543). X-Trace-Id header matches body.error.traceId, requestId is consistent, code/http_status/action_kind/messageKey prefix/headers all conform — but traceCode integrity fails, so under TRACE_INTEGRITY lens with DEFAULT REFUTE this envelope must be refuted.

parsability.reason: Envelope contains all must_have_fields (code, message, messageKey, traceId, traceCode, timestamp, action). Spec anchors satisfied: code=DB_UNAVAILABLE, http_status=503, action.kind=retry, messageKey has "errors.system" prefix, and all three response headers (X-Trace-Id, X-Request-Id, X-Relay-Health) present with X-Relay-Health=degraded enabling the global banner per error-handling.md §4.1.5. resolveError() in web/src/lib/api-client.ts would cleanly parse error.code for surface routing (banner per §4.3.3), error.traceCode "R-AGJV" for <ErrorDetails> copyable display, error.action.kind="retry" with after=5 for Retry CTA, and messageKey for i18n lookup. Headers mirror envelope IDs (X-Trace-Id matches error.traceId, X-Request-Id matches error.requestId). No parser-confusing shapes — JSON is well-formed, no field type mismatches, no nested error/error confusion. Matches the §13 postmortem example almost exactly.
